objective: Classify incoming unstructured information (meeting notes, research findings, bug reports, etc.) and route each item to its correct destination.

decision_points.decision: What category does each item belong to?
branches: Decision — a choice that was made → route to tasks/decisions.md
Lesson — a mistake, surprise, or pattern → route to tasks/lessons.md
Task — something that needs to be done → route to project task tracker
Reference — useful information to keep → route to relevant doc
Noise — not actionable, not worth keeping → skip

decision_points.decision: Is the classification of an item clear or ambiguous?
branches: Classification is clear — route the item to its destination
Classification is uncertain — ask the user rather than guessing

decision_points.decision: Which specific document should a Reference item be routed to?
branches: Relevant destination doc is identifiable — route there
Relevant destination doc is unclear — ask the user

decision_points.decision: What is the completion status of the triage?
branches: DONE_WITH_CONCERNS — Completed with issues to note
BLOCKED — Cannot proceed, state the blocker
NEEDS_CONTEXT — Missing information needed to continue

ask_nodes.question: What is the source of information to triage (file, clipboard, conversation history, inbox)?
ask_nodes.condition: Only if the source is not specified or is ambiguous

ask_nodes.question: Where should this item be routed? I'm unsure of its classification.
ask_nodes.condition: When unsure where something belongs — per the rule: ask the user rather than guessing

ask_nodes.question: Which document should this reference item be routed to?
ask_nodes.condition: When a Reference item has no obvious destination document

ask_nodes.question: Which project task tracker should this task be added to?
ask_nodes.condition: When the destination task tracker is not clear or multiple projects exist

success_criteria: Every input item is accounted for — routed, explicitly deferred with a reason, or explicitly skipped; none silently ignored
Items are classified into the correct categories (Decision, Lesson, Task, Reference, Noise)
All routed items have assertion-style titles (e.g., 'JWT cookie auth bridges Django sessions cleanly' not 'Authentication notes')
A clear triage summary report is produced showing counts and destinations for each category
Ambiguous items were clarified with the user rather than guessed
failure_criteria: Items are silently ignored or dropped without being routed, deferred, or skipped
Items are misclassified (e.g., a decision logged as a task)
Vague or descriptive titles used instead of assertion-style titles
Uncertain classifications are guessed at instead of asking the user
No summary report is produced at the end
The source of inputs is assumed incorrectly without confirming with the user
Status is reported as DONE when items were actually unresolved
